hero.alt: A close-up of a green praying mantis perched on a branch
hero.caption: Praying mantises are named for their unique front legs, which they fold as if praying.

habitat.fileTitle: File:Krems ad Donau Rehberg Europaschutzgebiet Kamp-und Kremstal Mantis-5549.jpg
habitat.alt: Praying mantis blending in with dry grasses in its natural grassland habitat
habitat.caption: They live in warm grasslands, meadows, and gardens where they can easily blend in.

diet.fileTitle: File:European mantis (Mantis religiosa) eating a cicada 02.jpg
diet.alt: A praying mantis holding and eating a large cicada in its grasp
diet.caption: As carnivores, mantises eat other insects and are excellent garden helpers by controlling pests.

baby.fileTitle: File:Praying mantis (Mantis religiosa) ootheca Cap Bon.jpg
baby.alt: A protective frothy brown praying mantis ootheca attached to a twigs
baby.caption: Baby mantises hatch from a hard, protective egg case called an ootheca.

family.fileTitle: File:Mantis religiosa (Mantidae) (Praying Mantis) - (nymph), Otok Krk, Croatia.jpg
family.alt: A tiny green praying mantis nymph resting on a stem
family.caption: Young mantises, or nymphs, look just like adults but are much smaller and lack wings.

range.alt: A brown praying mantis crawling upward on a thin twig
range.caption: Praying mantises can be found on several continents, including Europe, Asia, Africa, and North America.

size.alt: A green praying mantis showing its body length on a vertical flower bud
size.caption: Adult European mantises grow to about 2 to 3 inches (5 to 8 cm) in length.

closeup.alt: Close-up of a praying mantis's face showing large compound eyes and antennae
closeup.caption: A mantis has large eyes and a flexible neck that allows it to turn its head 180 degrees.

fun-fact.alt: Praying mantis camouflaged among green leaves and stems
fun-fact.caption: Mantises use camouflage to hide from birds and frogs, as well as to sneak up on prey.

core-habitat.alt: A green praying mantis standing alert on a leaf in a garden
core-habitat.caption: Meadows and home gardens provide a perfect habitat for hunting prey.

core-diet.alt: A praying mantis waiting patiently on a twig to strike
core-diet.caption: They are ambush predators, standing motionless until prey comes close.

core-family.alt: A praying mantis turning its head to look directly at the camera
core-family.caption: European mantises are solitary animals and defend their hunting territory.

core-baby.alt: A tiny newly hatched mantis nymph climbing a green plant stem
core-baby.caption: Hundreds of tiny nymphs hatch from a single ootheca in spring.

core-range.alt: A brown praying mantis climbing green foliage
core-range.caption: They have successfully adapted to many climates worldwide.
